one_time_description: Ammunition and Twine Settlement funds received in 2022; Agricultural Benefits Settlement funds of $57,793,438 received in prior year 2021
economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted FNDF revenues and band-owned business revenues. First Nation owns 424337 Alberta Ltd., Horse Lake Oil and Gas Corporation, Horse Lake Industry Relations Corporation, Horse Lake First Nation Investment Management Board (which owns 959447 Alberta Ltd., Clear Hills Development Corporation, 2035555 Alberta Ltd., and Dash Energy Services Ltd.), and HLFN Oilfield Construction Corp. Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust fully consolidated.
notes: Audit is qualified due to investments in related First Nation entities being accounted for using the modified equity method rather than full consolidation - auditors could not obtain sufficient financial records for these material investments. Five GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. One subsidiary (Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust) fully consolidated. Ammunition and Twine Settlement of $2,192,724 received in 2022 is a one-time item. Prior year 2021 included a massive $57,793,438 Agricultural Benefits Settlement which inflated prior year revenue and surplus significantly. Accumulated surplus includes $22,933,542 equity in Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust and $30,343,654 equity in First Nation entities. Accounts payable includes $11,655,000 Agricultural Benefits Minor's Payable. Total government transfers estimated as ISC + ISET + ISC Health + CMHC subsidy + CMHC special + CMHC RHI + Government of Alberta = 5734899+301439+3129572+312449+665000+1422000+184042+1041253 from deferred revenue context; used sum of identifiable government transfer lines.
